scenario: vague input — skill clarifies before stress-testing

input.idea: I feel like our deployment process is too slow and error-prone

expected_behavior: Skill should NOT reject the vague input. Instead: (1) ask clarifying questions one at a time (what is painful, what would change if solved, what's been tried), (2) once a direction emerges (e.g., 'we want to add automated smoke tests before deploy'), transition naturally to decision tree expansion, (3) exhaust all branches — no TBD leaves, (4) output decision map with IN/OUT/Deferred scope boundary

scenario: concrete plan — skill goes directly to branch tree exhaustion

input.plan: We'll add a caching layer in front of the database. When a request comes in, check cache first; if miss, query DB and populate cache. TTL is 5 minutes.

expected_behavior: Skill should skip clarification and go directly to decision tree. Surfaces hidden decisions: (1) cache invalidation on write? (2) cache key strategy? (3) what happens on cache failure — serve stale or fail? All branches must resolve before decision map is written.

scenario: blocked — user cannot resolve a critical branch

input.plan: Migrate users from old auth to new auth system
input.unresolvable_conflict: Team cannot decide between big-bang vs gradual rollout — depends on budget approval not yet received

expected_behavior: Skill surfaces migration strategy as critical unresolved branch, proposes both options with trade-offs. When user confirms the decision is blocked by external factor, outputs BLOCKED status with specific decision needed.

scenario: should redirect — codebase exists and terminology audit is needed

input.context: We have existing code and CONTEXT.md, but new features added terms that aren't documented yet

expected_behavior: Skill should stop and redirect: 'You have an existing codebase — use /s0-grill-docs to audit terminology drift against CONTEXT.md.'
